build: Build #25 — 200 OK + text/html error page with styled warning banner

ui_result.user_experience: Identical to Build #22-#24. Gamma shows 1 empty card, theme selection area. No warning banner rendered despite HTML response containing styled warning.

console_errors: [PromptAndOutlineSettings] error generating outline: Unable to complete request

diagnosis.conclusion: Build #25 (200 OK + text/html error page) APF interception confirmed. The HTML error page was returned with 200 OK status but Gamma frontend ignores the response body content entirely. It expects SSE text/event-stream format and treats any non-SSE response as 'Unable to complete request' regardless of content-type or body.
diagnosis.key_finding: Gamma's EventSource/SSE parser discards ALL non-SSE responses. HTTP status code and content-type are irrelevant. The HTML error page body is never rendered or parsed by the frontend. Only SSE text/event-stream with proper 'data: ...' lines will be processed.
diagnosis.recommendation: The ONLY viable approach for Gamma warning delivery is: (1) Return SSE text/event-stream format with warning text embedded in data lines, OR (2) Client-side JavaScript injection to modify the DOM after Gamma's error handler fires.
